courses.title: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics
Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics

notes: All transfer applicants must satisfy University of California admissions eligibility requirements and campus admission selection criteria. All admission requirements must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility requirements and admission to UC Santa Cruz, please visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that ensures your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

The Earth and Planetary Sciences Department welcomes prospective transfer students who are prepared for the major. No major preparation courses are required prior to transfer; however, the following courses or their equivalents are recommended to ensure timely graduation: All of the following courses: ANTH 1: Introduction to Biological Anthropology ANTH 2: Introduction to Cultural Anthropology ANTH 3: Introduction to Archaeology One of the following options: MATH 11A: Calculus with Applications AND MATH 11B: Calculus with Applications MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND MATH 19B: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics Plus one of the following options: EART 5: California Geology AND EART 5L: California Geology Laboratory EART 10: Geologic Principles AND EART 10L: Geologic Principles Laboratory EART 20: Environmental Geology AND EART 20L: Environmental Geology Laboratory Five lower-division science courses taken from the following list (note the lecture course and its associated lab course count as one course towards the five): BIOL 20A: Cell and Developmental Biology BIOE 20B: Development and Physiology BIOE 20C: Ecology and Evolution CHEM 3A: General Chemistry* CHEM 3B/3BL: General Chemistry and Lab* CHEM 3C/3CL: General Chemistry and Lab* CHEM 4A/4AL: Advanced General Chemistry: Molecular Structure and Reactivity* CHEM 4B/4BL: Advanced General Chemistry: Molecular Structure and Reactivity* PHYS 5A/5L: Introduction to Physics I and Introduction to Physics I Laboratory PHYS 5B/5M: Introduction to Physics II and Introduction to Physics II Laboratory PHYS 6A/6L: Introductory Physics I and Introductory Physics I Laboratory PHYS 6B/6M: Introductory Physics II and Introductory Physics II Laboratory *See "General Chemistry Series" below for important information Please visit the Earth & Planetary Sciences Department website to learn more about this major.

The UC Santa Cruz Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A-Level examination credit policies may be found in the UC Santa Cruz General Catalog . AP, IBH, and A-level exams may or may not satisfy course requirements, depending on the specific exam.

This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. Although not required for admission, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division preparatory courses as possible from among those listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section before transferring to UC Santa Cruz. Doing so will help you make timely progress toward degree completion. Additionally, completing the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (IGETC) is encouraged, especially for non-screening majors in the Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences Divisions. While not required for admission, IGETC completion will facilitate timely graduation. For more information on incorporating IGETC into your academic plan, please consult with your community college counselor.

Prior to July 1, 2023, UC Santa Cruz's General Chemistry series was offered as Chemistry 1A, 1B and 1C, with associated laboratories Chemistry 1M and 1N. Starting in the 2023-2024 academic year, this series was replaced by two new distinct series: Chemistry 3A, 3B and 3C, with labs Chemistry 3BL and 3CL; and Chemistry 4A and 4B with labs Chemistry 4AL and 4BL. Prospective transfer students who started working toward the Chemistry 1 series prior to the 2024-2025 academic year may complete equivalent courses as originally approved, and their articulated coursework will fulfill General Chemistry course requirements for this major. It is strongly recommended that community college students complete all General Chemistry coursework for their intended major at one institution prior to transfer to UC Santa Cruz.
